metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_1774
metadata.comment:
Rationale: 2/11/2017 Pim
- Authors show N-cadherin labeling of the synaptic cleft outside of the area defined by the active zone and PSD (Fig.7E). The authors interpret this labeling as to originate from N-cadherin proteins intergral to the plasm membranes of the pre- and postsynaptic compartments. They do this based on the observations that 1) N-cadherin interacts with intracellular alphaN-catenin protein (Fig.8) and that 2) alphaN-catenin is present at the interface of the presynaptic as well as the postsynaptic plasma membrane at a region reminiscent of the N-cadherin area (Fig.7F; no double staining was used).
- Based on the known transmembrane topology of the protein, the "integral component of ... membrane" term was chosen.. Experimental description: Materials and methods. "For adult mice those older than 1 mo were used.........The days when the animals were born was regarded as postnatal day 0. The study also used chicken tissue but figures for the annotation involved mouse tissue.
